scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a written Agreement to Sell regarding Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. Under the terms of the agreement, the Defendant was obligated to execute the sale deed upon receipt of the balance consideration on or before 10.07.2024. A copy of the Agreement to Sell is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

5. In performance of her obligations, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract and pay the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- as per the agreed timeline. The proof of payment is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

6. The Plaintiff holds the necessary funds and arrangements to complete the transaction and has consistently expressed her intention to proceed with the sale in accordance with the terms of the Agreement to Sell.

BREACH PARTICULARS

7. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the property. This communication constitutes a clear refusal to honor the contractual obligations undertaken by the Defendant. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

8. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed as per the agreement. The Defendant failed to comply with the notice and maintained his refusal to perform the contract. A copy of the legal notice and postal receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

DAMAGES

9. Due to the Defendant's breach and refusal to execute the sale deed, the Plaintiff has suffered direct loss and prejudice. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation for the loss sustained due to the non-performance of the contract, quantified at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-, which represents the direct and natural consequence of the Defendant's failure to convey the title as agreed.

LEGAL BASIS

10. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

11. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

12. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for specific performance to accrue.

13. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

14. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

15. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE

16. The Plaintiff states that the suit is subject to mediation under Order XXIII Rule 1A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The Plaintiff is willing to participate in mediation if referred by this Hon'ble Court.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} in favour of the Plaintiff;
(b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
(c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
(d) Award costs of the suit;
(e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt with UTR: HDFC20240110987654

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 16 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Commercial Court (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT Bengaluru

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: Company,
Residing at: MG Road, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: Limited Liability Partnership,
Residing at: Whitefield, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{AMOUNT}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when contract is broken; or for continuing breach, when it ceases. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. {{MEDIATION_STATUS -- CONFIRM: (a) Was pre-institution mediation conducted? If yes, provide mediation authority, date, and outcome certificate.
(b) If not, is urgent interim relief being sought? Section 12A compliance is mandatory for commercial suits unless urgent interim relief is sought.}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On {{DATE_OF_CONTRACT}}, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a written agreement regarding {{CONTRACT_DETAILS}}, copy whereof is filed as Annexure P-1.
7. The Plaintiff duly performed all obligations required on its part under the agreement, specifically {{PLAINTIFF_PERFORMANCE_DETAILS}}.
8. Under the terms of the agreement, the Defendant was required to {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATION}}.

BREACH PARTICULARS

9. The Defendant failed to perform the contractual obligations on {{DATE_OF_BREACH}}.
10. The Plaintiff issued legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} calling upon the Defendant to perform its obligations, but the Defendant did not comply. A copy of the notice is filed as Annexure P-2.

DAMAGES

11. By reason of the Defendant's failure to perform, the Plaintiff has suffered actual and direct financial loss.
12. The Plaintiff quantifies the said loss at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-, and the computation with supporting material is set out in Annexure P-3.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

16. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

17. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

18. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

    The interest is justified because Pre-suit interest on delayed payment: contractual rate if stipulated, otherwise 12-18% p.a. simple interest. Pendente lite interest under Order XX Rule 11 CPC (NOT Section 34 CPC). Post-decree future interest under Section 34 CPC. S.73 ICA: compensation for loss naturally arising from breach — interest on delayed payment is a standard head.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages for breach of contract as particularised in the Schedule of Damages annexed hereto;
(b) Award pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems just from the date of suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 CPC, and future interest from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 CPC;
(c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
(d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual and direct financial loss
     Amount claimed: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-
     Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
     Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment

Annexure D — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure E — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 18 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

STATEMENT OF TRUTH

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff / authorised representative of the Plaintiff herein, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that the contents of the above plaint and the facts stated in paragraphs 1 to 18 are true to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

I understand that I shall be liable to proceedings under law for making a false statement.

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.
